title: How to Move Between Condos in Addis Without Hassle
excerpt: Condo-to-condo: dual building rules, aligned timelines, labeling, routing, two gate lists, deposits, staggered vs same-day, communication script.
content:
How to move between condos in Addis without hassle

A condo-to-condo move means two sets of rules: exit procedures at origin and entry procedures at destination. Hassle happens when only one building is coordinated. Treat both as equal projects.

Dual checklist mindset

  • Origin: move-out notice, elevator booking, deposit walkthrough expectations.
  • Destination: move-in reservation, key collection, insurance forms, orientation rules.

Align timelines

If destination allows loading only after a specific hour, tell your mover when requesting quotes. Misaligned keys and elevators are the top cause of standby charges.

One inventory, two floor plans

Label boxes with destination room names that match the new unit layout. Tape a printed plan on doors at the new condo so unload stays fast.

Truck routing between towers

Share both addresses and a realistic drive-time buffer. Traffic between corridors can separate crews from the second booking window.

Security IDs for both gates

Submit crew lists to both compounds. Names must match on both sides if companies differ.

Valuables and documents

Keep handover papers, keys, and personal valuables in one bag that never goes on the truck.

Cleaning and deposit strategy

Photo origin walls before the last box; schedule destination clean lightly before heavy furniture if dust from construction is heavy.

Hybrid same-day versus staggered

Same-day both ends saves money when windows align. Staggered days help if one building forbids overlap or keys arrive late—choose consciously.

Communication script

  • Morning: confirm truck ETA and both elevator windows.
  • Midday: text destination security if arrival shifts.
  • Before leaving origin: final sweep and key handoff plan.

Storage between closings

If keys misalign, short-term inventoried storage beats stacking boxes in a hallway.

Roommate or family splits

Initials on boxes; one payer on the invoice to avoid sidewalk arguments.

Parking and loading at both ends

Confirm truck access rules for each compound—if one site only allows shuttles, say so before quote day.

Fragile and same-day unload priority

Mark cartons for kitchen glass and electronics to unload before purely decorative items if the second window is tight.

Employer or finance documentation

If relocation is reimbursed, ask your mover early about VAT-ready invoices so you are not chasing paperwork across two handovers.

Conclusion

Moving between condos without hassle is mostly bilateral planning: two buildings, two rule sets, one disciplined crew, and honest time buffers. Nail that symmetry and the day stays linear.
author: Active Movers Team
